neighborhoods.name: Round Rock
neighborhoods.description: Family-friendly suburb north of Austin along I-35 with excellent schools, H-E-B Plus flagship stores, Dell Technologies headquarters, and a growing downtown district
neighborhoods.character: Suburban family-oriented, tech corridor, chain retail, sports parks, rapidly growing

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes, newer subdivisions, townhomes, and apartment complexes

neighborhoods.character_notes: H-E-B Plus and multiple grocery options. Dell HQ brings tech jobs. Round Rock Express minor league baseball. Excellent Williamson County schools. Old Settlers Park and Brushy Creek trail system. 20 min to downtown Austin without traffic.
neighborhoods.groceryAccess: Excellent. H-E-B Plus (flagship), H-E-B, Costco, Walmart Supercenter, Sprouts, Trader Joe's all within short drive.
neighborhoods.healthcareAccess: Baylor Scott & White Round Rock, St. David's Round Rock Medical Center. Multiple urgent care and primary care options.
pros: Lower cost of living than central Austin
Excellent schools (Round Rock ISD)
H-E-B Plus flagship store
Low crime rate
Growing restaurant and retail scene
No state income tax
cons: Car-dependent — minimal public transit
I-35 traffic congestion to/from Austin
Suburban sprawl — limited walkability
Summer heat with less tree cover than central Austin
Limited nightlife and cultural events compared to Austin proper

neighborhoods.name: Cedar Park
neighborhoods.description: Growing northwest suburb near Lakeline Mall with a suburban feel, good schools, and easy access to Lake Travis and the Hill Country
neighborhoods.character: Suburban residential, family-friendly, Lakeline Mall area, Hill Country access, newer development
neighborhoods.character_notes: Lakeline Mall area retail hub. Cedar Park Center (H-E-B Center) hosts Texas Stars hockey and Austin Spurs basketball. MetroRail Red Line has Lakeline station. Close to Lake Travis for boating and swimming. Leander ISD schools.
neighborhoods.groceryAccess: Good. H-E-B, Costco (nearby), Walmart, Sprouts, Randalls within short drive.
neighborhoods.healthcareAccess: Cedar Park Regional Medical Center (Baylor Scott & White). Multiple urgent care clinics. Specialist access may require travel to Austin.
pros: Family-friendly with good schools (Leander ISD)
Close to Lake Travis and Hill Country recreation
MetroRail Red Line connection to downtown Austin
Growing dining and retail options
Lower property taxes than Travis County (Williamson County)
Active community events and parks
cons: Car-dependent for most errands
US 183 traffic congestion during peak hours
Limited walkability outside Lakeline area
Fewer cultural amenities than central Austin
Rapid growth straining infrastructure

neighborhoods.name: South Austin (78745/78748)
neighborhoods.description: Eclectic, laid-back neighborhoods south of Lady Bird Lake near Zilker Park, known for food trucks, local businesses, and the 'Keep Austin Weird' vibe
neighborhoods.character: Eclectic, artsy, food truck culture, Zilker Park adjacent, mix of old Austin and new development

housing.predominantType: Mix of older ranch homes, newer condos, duplexes, and apartment complexes

neighborhoods.character_notes: Closest to Zilker Park, Barton Springs Pool, and Lady Bird Lake hike-and-bike trail. South Lamar and South Congress (SoCo) corridors with restaurants and shops. Food truck parks. More walkable than suburbs. Strong local business culture. Gentrifying rapidly.
neighborhoods.groceryAccess: Good. H-E-B (multiple locations including South Congress and William Cannon), Wheatsville Food Co-op, Trader Joe's, Sprouts nearby.
neighborhoods.healthcareAccess: St. David's South Austin Medical Center. Multiple primary care and urgent care clinics. Close to downtown hospital network.
pros: Walking distance to Zilker Park, Barton Springs, Lady Bird Lake
Vibrant food truck and local restaurant scene
More walkable than suburban areas
Strong sense of community and local culture
CapMetro bus routes more frequent than suburbs
Dog-friendly patios and parks everywhere
cons: Higher cost of living than outer suburbs
Gentrification pushing prices up rapidly
MoPac and I-35 traffic congestion
Older housing stock may need maintenance
Parking can be difficult near SoCo and Zilker during events
Flash flood risk in some low-lying areas

neighborhoods.name: Georgetown
neighborhoods.description: Charming small city north of Austin with a historic Victorian downtown square, Sun City 55+ active adult community, and a strong retiree-friendly identity
neighborhoods.character: Small-city historic, retiree-friendly, Sun City 55+ community, Victorian downtown square

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes, Sun City 55+ community homes, historic downtown properties

neighborhoods.character_notes: Sun City Texas is one of the largest 55+ active adult communities in the US (Del Webb). Historic Georgetown Square with shops, restaurants, and First Friday events. Blue Hole Park swimming. Georgetown municipal utility (lower electricity rates). Named 'Most Beautiful Town Square in Texas.'
neighborhoods.groceryAccess: Good. H-E-B, Walmart Supercenter, Randalls. Central Market and specialty stores require trip to Austin/Round Rock.
neighborhoods.healthcareAccess: St. David's Georgetown Hospital, Baylor Scott & White Georgetown. Growing medical services but specialists may require travel to Austin.
pros: Sun City 55+ community with extensive amenities (golf, pools, clubs)
Beautiful historic downtown square
Lower cost of living than Austin proper
Georgetown municipal utility — competitive electricity rates
Low crime rate, strong community feel
Blue Hole Park, Lake Georgetown recreation
cons: 30-40 min drive to downtown Austin
Very car-dependent — no meaningful public transit
Limited dining and entertainment compared to Austin
Fewer specialist healthcare options locally
Rapid growth changing small-town character
Summer heat with limited shade in newer developments

neighborhoods.name: Pflugerville
neighborhoods.description: Diverse, affordable, and rapidly growing northeast suburb with a mix of established neighborhoods and new master-planned communities
neighborhoods.character: Diverse suburban, affordable, fast-growing, family-friendly, community-oriented

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes, newer master-planned communities, townhomes, apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: One of Austin's most diverse suburbs. Pflugerville Pfarmers Market. Lake Pflugerville for fishing and trails. Hendrickson High School well-regarded. SH 130 toll road provides bypass of I-35. Stone Hill Town Center retail hub. German heritage (original settlers).
neighborhoods.groceryAccess: Good. H-E-B (multiple locations), Walmart Supercenter, Asian grocery stores reflecting diverse community. Costco in nearby Round Rock.
neighborhoods.healthcareAccess: Ascension Seton Pflugerville clinic. Multiple urgent care options. Major hospitals in Austin and Round Rock within 15-20 min drive.
pros: Most affordable option in Austin metro
Culturally diverse community
Lake Pflugerville recreation
SH 130 toll road avoids I-35 congestion
Good schools (Pflugerville ISD)
Active community events (Deutschen Pfest, Pfarmers Market)
cons: Car-dependent — very limited public transit
SH 130 tolls add up for frequent commuters
Fewer dining and entertainment options than Austin
Rapid growth straining roads and infrastructure
No local hospital — must travel to Austin or Round Rock
Can feel disconnected from Austin's cultural scene
